attempt: Quantify the countermodel pressure against finite Li-prefix proof routes: as surrogate off-critical zero height grows, its first-prefix effect becomes arbitrarily small in the tested family.
obstruction: No fixed finite prefix can rule out sufficiently high off-critical behavior by itself. A proof must include a theorem that quantifies over the entire zero set or the entire Li sequence.
candidate_theorem: For every off-critical zero, a height-uniform detector bound produces a contradiction in the Li/kernel sequence before any finite certificate is promoted.
next_experiment: Turn the empirical height table into a symbolic N,H,Re(rho) inequality.
claim_boundary: No RH proof and no certified RH counterexample.
